title: Node Process, Event Loop, Modules, Configuration, and Lifecycle · Transfer Build
summary: An independent service team receives a different tenant, traffic, trust, persistence, and failure profile and transfers node process, event loop, modules, configuration, and lifecycle into a new process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn.
Configure ESM, CommonJS interop, package exports, file extensions, and NodeNext resolution without relying on accidental development-loader behavior.
Parse, validate, normalize, redact, and freeze configuration once before acquiring resources or accepting traffic.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Work Queues
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 7254 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Work Queues.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace call stack, timers, pending callbacks, poll, check, close callbacks, microtasks, and next-tick work without assuming one request owns one event-loop turn. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Lab Event Loop Work Queues, keep Event Loop Work Queues connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Signal Drain Exit symptom in process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model during this lab case.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Node.js 24 HTTP API — Controls messages, streams, headers, request and response behavior, server options, timeouts, close methods, upgrades, parsers, and connection lifecycle.

hints: For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Signal Drain Exit, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Signal Drain Exit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Signal Drain Exit, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 2206 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.

For Node and Express server Test an acceptance boundary · Lab Signal Drain Exit, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Calling process.exit immediately after receiving SIGTERM gives pending responses time to finish.

stimulus.title: Signal Drain Exit failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Calling process.exit immediately after receiving SIGTERM gives pending responses time to finish.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.

options.text: Keep the plausible Signal Drain Exit output and hide the violated process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Signal Drain Exit.

For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Signal Drain Exit happy path, omit method, authority, credential, byte, time, redirect, retry, and cleanup boundaries, then accept plausible output. This does not satisfy Signal Drain Exit for process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Signal Drain Exit condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Signal Drain Exit
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Signal Drain Exit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab team handles case 3825 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate configuration, readiness, admission, and signal-driven draining; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then fail the canary while the old revision drains and prove rollback remains safe. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Signal Drain Exit.
steps.why: Retrieving Signal Drain Exit through the concrete process, event-loop, configuration, and lifecycle state model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Node and Express server Defend the transferred model · Lab Signal Drain Exit, keep Signal Drain Exit connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle termination signals with idempotent draining, bounded cleanup, rejection tracking, exit-code ownership, and forced-stop evidence.
